phrases.cultural_note: Koulouria are small, ring-shaped breads often sold by weight in Greek bakeries. Customers typically select and weigh them directly at the counter.

phrases.text_local: Πού περιμένω για ψωμί ή γλυκά;
phrases.cultural_note: In Greek bakeries, queues are typically organized by the type of item customers intend to purchase, e.g., bread or pastries.

likely_response.local: Ψωμί: σειρά φούρνου. Γλυκά: βιτρίνα δεξιά.
likely_response.translation_en: For bread, queue in front of the oven. For pastries, queue at the display case with koulouria and baklava.

responses.english: For bread, queue in front of the oven. For pastries, queue at the display case with koulouria and baklava.
responses.local: Ψωμί: σειρά φούρνου. Γλυκά: βιτρίνα δεξιά.

phrases.cultural_note: In small bakeries, savory pastries like spanakopita are typically kept behind the main counter. Customers often need to ask staff for access.

likely_response.local: Αριστερά στην προθήκη, δίπλα στα κουλούρια.
likely_response.translation_en: In the display case by the oven.

responses.english: In the display case by the oven.
responses.local: Αριστερά στην προθήκη, δίπλα στα κουλούρια.

phrases.intent_en: Exact change preferred for coffee bundle?
phrases.cultural_note: Bakeries in Greece often prefer exact change in the morning due to high customer traffic and limited time for transactions.
Tsoureki is a traditional Easter sweet bread, but it is commonly sold year-round in Greek bakeries as a festive treat.
